title: jasminum smilacifolium
intro: Jasminum smilacifolium, also known as the Indian jasmine or Poison Ashoka, is a climbing plant native to the tropical forests of India and Sri Lanka. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Jasminum smilacifolium include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
